fresh_facts.claim: President Trump announced a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz effective April 14, 2026, targeting Iranian oil exports after 21-hour peace talks in Islamabad collapsed.
fresh_facts.source: Multiple sources including Hindustan Times and CBS News

fresh_facts.claim: US warships USS Michael Murphy and USS Frankie Eie Peterson Jr. transited the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend to clear Iranian mines, marking the first such crossing since the war began.
IRGC forces released footage on April 11-12, 2026 of warning a US Navy destroyer in the Strait of Hormuz, claiming missile and drone locks forced a retreat after a radio exchange.
US CENTCOM denies any confrontation occurred with Iranian forces in the Strait of Hormuz, contradicting Iranian claims.
Iran threatens a severe response to US warships nearing its waters amid the standoff in the Strait of Hormuz.
A two-week ceasefire between Iran, the US, and Israel mediated by Pakistan is holding unevenly amid mutual accusations of violations as of April 13, 2026.
US Vice President JD Vance, envoy Steve Witkoff, and Jared Kushner are leading upcoming US-Iran talks in Islamabad to solidify ceasefire terms including reopening the Strait of Hormuz.
Iran's 10-point plan and the US's 15-point plan remain in disagreement regarding ceasefire negotiation frameworks.
President Trump warned via social media that US forces will remain in the region until full compliance, or the real shooting starts.
Trump postponed strikes on Iran's power grid after what he characterized as constructive talks.
Iran accuses the US of violations via Israeli strikes on Hezbollah in Lebanon and Iranian territory during the ceasefire, which the US denies.
Parliament Speaker Mohammad-Bagher Ghalibaf highlighted Iranian accusations of US violations via Israeli strikes as a key ceasefire dispute.
Iran's ambassador to Pakistan announced then deleted a delegation's arrival related to ceasefire negotiations.
President Masoud Pezeshkian faces intense hardliner backlash for signaling conditional war-end willingness, with critics branding him a traitor.
Former Iranian officials Mohammad Javad Zarif and Hassan Rouhani face hardliner calls for arrest over peace signaling.
Tensions between President Pezeshkian and IRGC chief Ahmad Vahidi have led to a complete political deadlock.
The IRGC is assuming key state functions amid political tensions with President Pezeshkian.
President Pezeshkian appointed IRGC figure Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr as Supreme National Security Council secretary under Guard pressure.
Iranian state media adopted a harsher anti-dissent tone and issued warnings against revealing officials' locations.
Iran's economy shows structural collapse with currency devaluation and a shift in monetary regime amid war costs.
Iranian officials frame Strait of Hormuz control as a financial asset in response to economic pressures.
Global shocks from Hormuz disruptions are exacerbating Iran's economic volatility amid eroded checks and balances.
Iranian state broadcaster frames domestic protests as enemy warfare and has adopted a harsher tone against dissent.
Recent Iranian protests are described as the regime's most serious unrest ever, threatening its character.
A CBS poll shows 64% US disapproval of Trump's Iran handling as of mid-April 2026.
US military carriers remain approximately 1,000 miles from the Persian Gulf, limiting leverage against Iran's drone capabilities according to expert analysis.

regime_fall.current_status: A fragile two-week ceasefire mediated by Pakistan is holding unevenly as Trump announced a naval blockade effective April 14 targeting Iranian oil exports, while intense internal power struggles between President Pezeshkian and hardline IRGC leadership create succession uncertainty amid economic structural collapse and unprecedented domestic unrest.
regime_fall.last_major_event: President Trump announced a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz effective April 14, 2026, targeting Iranian oil exports after 21-hour peace talks in Islamabad collapsed.
